methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

data.narrative:
Cailabs, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised €57M+, is valued within a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies: Sierra Space ($8.0B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), Isar Aerospace ($1.0B), and Loft Orbital ($1.0B).

The peer-median method anchors valuation at $1.1B, reflecting the median of the five-company cohort. This approach is appropriate given the availability of direct-stage and sector comparables; public-comp anchoring was not applied because Cailabs' revenue is not on file, precluding application of the 42.9x median P/S multiple observed across six public space and aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL). The peer range spans $1.0B to $8.0B, with the wide upper bound driven by Sierra Space's outlier valuation.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $978M to $1.3B, centered on $1.1B. Key uncertainties include whether Cailabs' market positioning and commercialization progress warrant alignment with the median, or whether it tracks closer to the lower-quartile peers (Isar, Loft Orbital) or the higher-end outlier. The single applicable method limits triangulation robustness; resolution of revenue disclosure or market traction would enable cross-validation via public-comp metrics.
